name: Rellim Cindralis

attacks.name: Poison Arrow
attacks.notes: Arrows coated with a basic poison

attacks.name: Fire Arrow
attacks.notes: Arrows wrapped in cloth soaked with oil, ignited on fire

specialEquipment.name: Windwalker +1
specialEquipment.description: An Elven longbow found in the hidden Crypt of Irillis.

traits: Slow to trust, but would die for a friend
ideals: Family. Blood runs thicker than water.
bonds: My loyalty to my Ancestry is unwavering.
flaws: I hide a secret that could ruin my family ancestry forever.

backstory:
The Shadow of Irillis
Long before the people of Aurelion whispered the name Ranger, he was Rellim Cindralis, a child of the high hills overlooking the skeletal ruins of Irillis. Among moss-slicked stone and shattered spires, his father taught him that survival was a language. He learned to read the land like a script, to move as a secret, and to wield knowledge with the same lethal precision as a blade. Through flickering firelight, Rellim discovered that Irillis was not merely a graveyard of stone, but a fallen sanctuary of scholars whose secrets predated the God King himself.
Their life was one of nomadic silence, until the silence broke.
The God King’s Marshals of Elandros descended like a fever in the night. From beneath the floorboards of their cabin, Rellim watched his world turn to ash. He saw his father fight with the grace of a storm, only to be bound by unnatural sorcery and executed by a figure in gold-masked armor. When the Marshals vanished, they took the body and left Rellim with nothing but a bloodstained floor and an ancient signet ring, the sole inheritance of a dead city.
At dawn, Rellim emerged not as a boy, but as a ghost. He abandoned the comfort of walls, trusting the wilderness over kings and shadows over men. He became a creature of the forest’s edge, haunted by the memory of a golden mask and guided by a legacy he was not yet ready to claim.
The Scholar’s Assistant
Seeking the only bridge to his past, Rellim traveled south to Thalmyre to find Durlin Nidavellir, a dwarven academic who wore the God King’s approval like a well-tailored coat. To the public, Durlin was a meticulous lecturer of approved history; in private, he was a guardian of the forbidden.
For seven years, Rellim lived in the heart of the enemy’s culture. He served as Durlin’s shadow, copying manuscripts, organizing archives, and sitting in the back of lecture halls. But in the quiet of sealed vaults, he studied the truth of Irillis, piecing together a history the God King had tried to burn from the world’s memory.
The Turning Point
The peace of the archives ended with a single slip of a page. Caught with a text he was never meant to touch, Rellim saw the weight of seven years vanish in an instant. With the Marshals closing in, Durlin urged him into the night, severing his last tie to safety.
Rellim fled north, aiming for the familiar cold of his homeland. But at an unremarkable crossroads on the King’s Road, fate pulled at his cloak. He chose the path to the right, turning away from the Dragon’s Breath Inn and stepping instead toward a destiny that would change the map of Aurelion forever.
